Deck Carry.canonical_display_pose: One complete normal skateboard is held securely under one arm or by one hand at the deck edge while both feet remain planted naturally on the floor.
required_checks: grip-tape top and truck-mounted underside are opposite faces
no trucks mounted on visible grip-tape face
board held under arm or by deck edge
no board balanced on wheel or tail

Push-Off.canonical_display_pose: One foot is planted on the plain black grip-tape top of a complete skateboard while the other foot pushes from the floor or has just lifted after the push; the illustrated underside is completely hidden.
required_checks: one foot planted on top grip tape
other foot pushing or freshly lifted
top surface is plain matte black grip tape
only thin natural laminated-wood edge visible
no artwork wrapping around deck edge

Pop-Up Ready.canonical_display_pose: Surfer and surfboard travel on one shared axis; the narrow pointed nose is forward and the broad tail, traction pad, and fins are rearward; both feet are supported in a diagonal stance, with the rear foot over the tail pad toward the heel-side rail and the front foot farther forward toward the toe-side rail.
required_checks: tail pad and fins at rear
rear foot offset toward heel-side rail
front foot offset toward toe-side rail
feet form a natural diagonal across the stringer
feet are not both centered on stringer
feet are not both on same side of board
visible deck or traction pad means all fins hidden on underside
regular stance means anatomical left foot forward
goofy stance means anatomical right foot forward
hips and shoulders aligned with board travel
athlete and board face the same direction
no mirrored rider opposing board travel

Binding Check.canonical_display_pose: Snowboard lies flat while the athlete crouches beside it with both boots outside the bindings and both hands checking the nearest binding; no crossed leg and no boot entering the opposite-side binding.
